title: Petrobras and Vale Move Forward With Partnership in Fuel Supply
content: Petrobras and Vale signed a contract to supply diesel to the mining company's operations in Minas Gerais. The agreement involves the supply of S10 diesel, produced by Petrobras, with the addition of the mandatory portion of biodiesel, currently at 15%. The agreement also provides for low-carbon business development opportunities, such as the possibility of buying and selling Diesel R (diesel with renewable content) and possible negotiations for the supply of HVO (Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil). Since 2023, the two companies have been collaborating on business partnerships for the development of low-carbon solutions. In 2024, an agreement was established for cooperation, including the tests of Diesel R5 (5% renewable content, in addition to the mandatory 15% of biodiesel) and bunker with 24% renewable portion. The commercial partnership with Vale is part of Petrobras' strategy to get closer to the end consumers of its products. According to the president of Petrobras, Magda Chambriard, "establishing this direct contact allows the company to better understand the needs of consumers and be more assertive in building solutions that generate value for customers and society. By offering high-performance fuels and also capable of collaborating with the companies' decarbonization goals, Petrobras improves its logistics structure and capacity to produce for customers of international relevance".
author: Analysts Account
